title: Define a struct
instruction:
Declare a struct with named fields. Create instances with field names.

Run this.
successMessage: Point bundles two f64 values with names. Access fields with dot notation.

title: Mutable struct instance
instruction:
To change a field, the variable must be `mut`.

Run this.
successMessage: Rust's mutability is all-or-nothing: the whole binding is mut or not. You cannot make individual fields selectively mutable.

title: Debug formatting
instruction:
`#[derive(Debug)]` generates a debug print. Use `{:?}` or `{:#?}`.

Run this.
successMessage: #[derive(Debug)] is free documentation. {:?} compact, {:#?} pretty. Essential while building programs.

title: Methods with impl
instruction:
Methods live in an `impl` block. The first parameter `&self` refers to the instance.

Run this.
successMessage: &self gives read-only access to the instance. Multiple methods can share an impl block.

title: Mutating methods
instruction:
Use `&mut self` to modify the struct in a method.

Run this.
successMessage: &mut self lets the method modify the struct. pop returns Option because the stack might be empty.

title: Associated functions
instruction:
Associated functions do not take `self`. Call them with `TypeName::function()`.

By convention, `new()` is the constructor.

Run this.
successMessage: Circle::new is the constructor. The shorthand Circle { radius } works because the field name matches the variable name.

title: Struct update syntax
instruction:
Reuse fields from an existing struct with `..existing`:

Run this.
successMessage: ..defaults fills in the fields not explicitly set. Great for configuration structs with many fields.

title: Tuple structs
instruction:
Tuple structs wrap values without field names. Access via `.0`, `.1`, etc.

Run this.
successMessage: You cannot accidentally pass Metres where Kilograms is expected. The compiler enforces it. This 'newtype pattern' adds type safety for free.
